divergences: Wallpaper is macOS-style abstract art with sharp angular color-shard boundaries (e.g. a hard diagonal cut between the top-right blue swoosh and the dark navy beneath it, and a similar dark/magenta valley on the right-edge strip around y=300-420css). One shard (top-right) was recreated with a clip-path polygon; the remaining shard edges are approximated with smooth measured gradients per edge (left/top/right/bottom sampled directly from the reference at 2x) rather than exact polygons, since the visible margin is only the ~44-50px frame around the window.

The window's backdrop-filter blur(120px) needs a plausible interior wallpaper field behind the whole window (not just the exposed margin) to produce the right frosted-glass wash; this interior field (.wp-wash) is a coarse radial-gradient approximation of the full desktop composition, not pixel-measured, since it is only ever seen through heavy blur.

Residual RMSE is dominated by JPEG compression block noise in the reference over what are smooth CSS gradients in the render (confirmed by inspecting a visually-smooth reference region that nonetheless diffs as high-frequency noise), plus ordinary text/icon edge anti-aliasing differences between the native app's captured render and Chromium's text rasterizer — both expected per the brief's fidelity ceiling for photographic/antialiasing detail.
